name: docker-log-since-window
description: DockerLogCaptureTool runs `docker logs -f --since <window>s` per container into .captures/ files, waits duration_seconds, SIGTERMs, and returns the last 8000 chars per container. The --since lookback is derived from the flow's own duration (duration * 3, floored at 60s), not 0s or a flat constant: workers run sequentially, so the flow's output predates capture start, and long flows need proportionally longer lookback.
invariants: --since must never be 0s while delegation stays sequential — 'from now on' would miss everything the flow already emitted
A docker_host override sets DOCKER_HOST only in the subprocess env; None means the ambient docker context
contracts: Returns JSON {container: {log_path, content}} — the shape anomaly_detector iterates over for error/slow-line scanning
